slug: 女性主义不是反男_它到底在说什么
title: 女性主义不是反男——它到底在说什么

outline.concept: Feminism critiques patriarchy—the system of gendered power structures—not men as individuals. Patriarchy names a set of rules about who gets to decide what; it doesn't mean 'men are evil.' You can hate the game without hating the players.
outline.query: 女权 仇男 误解 feminist anti-men 以为

outline.concept: Patriarchy prescribes rigid roles for men too: be the provider, never show weakness, always be in control. Men who cry, who choose caregiving, who fail to dominate get punished by the same system that subordinates women.
outline.query: 男生 坚强 不能哭 养家 压力 男人应该 masculine pressure

outline.concept: There's a difference between 'suffering under a system' and 'being oppressed by it.' Men can be hurt by patriarchy's rules without being structurally disadvantaged the way women are. Oppression isn't about individual pain—it's about which side of the power structure you're on.
outline.query: 不公平 制度 权力 受害 结构性 systemic disadvantage privilege

outline.concept: Feminism's endgame is gender liberation: dismantling the scripts so nobody has to perform a role they didn't choose. That's not zero-sum. Freeing women from 'must be submissive' and freeing men from 'must be dominant' are the same project.
outline.query: 性别角色 束缚 不想扮演 自由 liberation 解放 gender role
